message:
fix(cli): LazyLock regex in agents, defer-first blocked cache invalidation

Two improvements:

1. Regex optimization (agents.rs):
   - Promote blurb version regex from per-call Regex::new() to static
     LazyLock<Regex>, compiled once per process
   - Replace #[allow(clippy::missing_panics_doc)] with descriptive
     .expect() in the LazyLock initializer

2. Deferred cache invalidation strategy (mod.rs):
   - rebuild_blocked_cache_after_partial_mutation() now marks cache stale
     and returns immediately (defer-first) instead of eagerly rebuilding
   - Only falls back to rebuild_blocked_cache() if the stale marker
     write itself fails
   - finalize_batched_blocked_cache_refresh() same pattern: early return
     if already stale, defer if possible, rebuild only on marker failure
   - Tests switched to file-backed DB and updated assertions to match
     the new defer-first logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
